result.preview:
"Postgres query exectution error (ProgrammingError): (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedFunction) operator does not exist: text - text
LINE 3:        AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closeddate - createddate))) A...
                                                  ^
HINT:  No operator matches the given name and argument types. You might need to add explicit type casts.

[SQL: SELECT ownerid AS agent_id,
       COUNT(DISTINCT id) AS total_cases_handled,
       AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closeddate - createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds
FROM "Case"
WHERE createddate >= DATE '2023-05-02'
  AND createddate < DATE '2023-09-03'
  AND closeddate IS NOT NULL
  AND status = 'Closed'
  AND id IN (
      SELECT caseid__c
      FROM casehistory__c
      WHERE field__c = 'Owner'
      GROUP BY caseid__c
      HAVING COUNT(*) = 1
  )
GROUP BY ownerid
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT id) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;]
(Background on this error at: https://sqlalche.me/e/20/f405)"

args.query:
SELECT ownerid AS agent_id,
       COUNT(DISTINCT id) AS total_cases_handled,
       AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closeddate - createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds
FROM "Case"
WHERE createddate >= DATE '2023-05-02'
  AND createddate < DATE '2023-09-03'
  AND closeddate IS NOT NULL
  AND status = 'Closed'
  AND id IN (
      SELECT caseid__c
      FROM casehistory__c
      WHERE field__c = 'Owner'
      GROUP BY caseid__c
      HAVING COUNT(*) = 1
  )
GROUP BY ownerid
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT id) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;

args.query:
SELECT ownerid AS agent_id,
       COUNT(DISTINCT id) AS total_cases_handled,
       AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ((closeddate::timestamp) - (createddate::timestamp)))) AS avg_handle_seconds
FROM "Case"
WHERE createddate::date >= DATE '2023-05-02'
  AND createddate::date < DATE '2023-09-03'
  AND closeddate IS NOT NULL
  AND status = 'Closed'
  AND id IN (
      SELECT caseid__c
      FROM casehistory__c
      WHERE field__c = 'Owner'
      GROUP BY caseid__c
      HAVING COUNT(*) = 1
  )
GROUP BY ownerid
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT id) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT ownerid AS agent_id,
       COUNT(DISTINCT id) AS total_cases_handled,

FROM "Case"

result.preview: "I\u2019m unable to identify any agent that both (a) handled more than one closed case and (b) has valid created and closed timestamps in the past four months (2023-05-02 to 2023-09-02), because the Case table query over that period returns no matching records.\n\nGiven the available data and constraints, there is no agent Id I can return that satisfies your criteria."

args.answer:
I’m unable to identify any agent that both (a) handled more than one closed case and (b) has valid created and closed timestamps in the past four months (2023-05-02 to 2023-09-02), because the Case table query over that period returns no matching records.

Given the available data and constraints, there is no agent Id I can return that satisfies your criteria.

val_args.answer:
I’m unable to identify any agent that both (a) handled more than one closed case and (b) has valid created and closed timestamps in the past four months (2023-05-02 to 2023-09-02), because the Case table query over that period returns no matching records.

Given the available data and constraints, there is no agent Id I can return that satisfies your criteria.
